name: Maya the Freelance Brand Designer
summary: A freelance brand designer running a solo practice in design services. Bills clients hourly and treats every efficiency gain as direct profit. Has a strong interest in white-labeling features to maintain professional brand credibility with clients.
goals: Save 10+ minutes per project through faster templates and streamlined asset export
White-label the client share view to present projects under her own brand
Build persistent brand kits for each client to eliminate setup repetition
Consolidate client feedback into a single platform through comment threading
Grow monthly client volume by reducing per-project time investment
pains: Client share view shows the platform's own branding, undermining her professional presentation
Asset export is multi-step and slows down delivery on every project
Rebuilding color palettes and brand assets from scratch for repeat clients wastes billable time
Managing client feedback across email threads and screen shares is inefficient
Current tooling doesn't scale when taking on more clients simultaneously
motivations: Hourly billing model makes every minute of efficiency directly translatable to revenue
Professional branded presentation justifies premium pricing to clients
Reducing administrative friction to spend maximum time on creative work
Building a reputation for polished, seamless client delivery
objections: Won't pay for a tool that puts its own logo on client-facing deliverables
White-labeling as a paid upgrade requires calculating break-even against current workarounds
Switching costs are high if brand kits aren't portable
decision_triggers: White-label client share demo removing platform branding entirely
Time savings calculation demonstrating 10+ minutes saved per project
Peer designer recommendation based on client presentation quality
sample_quotes: I bill clients hourly so anything that saves me 10 minutes per project is worth real money. Can your client share view be white-labeled?
My clients shouldn't see what tool I'm using — they should see my brand.
If I'm rebuilding the brand kit every time, this tool isn't saving me anything.

journey_stages.mindset: Calculating whether efficiency gains justify switching costs
key_actions: Tests color picker and brand kit

journey_stages.mindset: Building first real client project to validate time savings

source_evidence.claim: Freelance Brand Designer running a solo practice
Client share view shows platform branding, undermining professional image
Billing hourly makes every minute saved directly profitable
Template browsing shows goal of faster project templating
